description_long: This house features a traditional half-timbered facade on the front, with exposed wooden beams set against a light stone or plaster background, evoking a classic European, likely French, architectural style. The steeply pitched gable roof is covered in weathered shingles, and three dormer windows project from the front, adding character and natural light to the upper floor. The front elevation includes large wooden doors and a set of windows, suggesting a mixed-use ground floor, possibly for commercial or storage purposes. The sides and rear of the house are plain, with smooth, light-colored walls and a simpler arrangement of windows, while the rear features a single door and several symmetrically placed windows. A prominent chimney rises from one side of the roof, indicating a fireplace or heating system inside. The overall structure is robust and solid, with materials and design elements typical of historic urban dwellings in medieval or Renaissance-era towns.
description: A traditional half-timbered house with a steep gable roof, dormer windows, and a prominent chimney, featuring a decorative front facade and plain sides and rear.
description_view_0: Front view showing the decorative half-timbered facade, three dormer windows, large wooden doors, and stonework details.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a plain, light-colored wall with a chimney and several roof windows, lacking decorative elements.
description_view_2: Rear view with a simple arrangement of windows and a single door, and a metal or slate roof surface.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the plain wall and roof windows, with the chimney visible.
attribute_window_arrangement: symmetrical rows on front, sparse and regular on sides and rear
attribute_entryway_design: Large wooden double doors at ground level, no porch or steps, framed by stone and timber elements
